caption: The video appears to be a financial analysis presentation focusing on the CBOE Volatility Index (VIX), also known as the "fear index," which measures market volatility and investor sentiment. The screen is divided into three sections, each displaying different time frames: daily, weekly, and monthly charts of the VIX. The daily chart shows recent price movements with various technical indicators like MACD and Stochastic Slow, highlighting key support and resistance levels. The weekly chart provides a broader view, noting significant events such as the Fed/virus impact and the Russia-Ukraine conflict, which influenced volatility. The monthly chart offers an even longer-term perspective, showing major peaks during the COVID-19 pandemic and other global events. The presenter likely discusses these trends, explaining how geopolitical and economic factors have affected market volatility over time.
